mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.05% represents an effective lower bound, forcing investors into equities to earn any positive real yield. This TINA effect supports equity valuations and produces positive forward returns. The mechanism is always active as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (mean > 0) during periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or if the correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent returns is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 10% triggers expectations of large-scale fiscal stimulus and accommodative monetary policy, which boost equity markets despite the weak current conditions. The threshold of 10% represents a psychological and policy-relevant level.
failure_condition: If in periods when UNRATE > 10%, forward 20-day returns are negative more often than positive, or if the mean return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Retail and food services sales below $450 billion signal severe consumer demand destruction, leading to corporate earnings downgrades and negative equity returns within a short horizon. The sharp drop in April 2020 to $404B activates this signal.
failure_condition: If when RSAFS < 450000, the 5-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index below 100 indicates manufacturing contraction and supply disruptions that impair corporate earnings. Low INDPRO predicts negative equity returns over a month as supply constraints persist.
failure_condition: If when INDPRO < 100, the 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Low CPI inflation combined with near-zero fed funds rate signals that the economy is in a deflationary trap with limited monetary ammunition. This combination depresses equity valuations as real interest rates rise and nominal growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (low CPI & low FEDFUNDS) does not predict negative 20-day returns in a statistically significant manner (p < 0.05 in a linear model) over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 10%), a decline in housing starts signals deepening economic weakness that outweighs any stimulus expectations, leading to negative equity returns. The negative impact of housing starts contraction is amplified during severe labor market distress.
failure_condition: If in the regime UNRATE > 10%, the conditional negative effect of low HOUST on 20-day returns is not significant (t-test p > 0.10) or if the direction of the interaction is reversed.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at a high level (over $19 trillion) reflects pre-pandemic economic strength and potential for faster recovery. Investors use this as a signal of corporate earning capacity, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as the economy rebounds.
failure_condition: If periods with GDPC1 above its median historical value do not produce positive average 60-day returns, or if the correlation between GDPC1 level and subsequent 60-day returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Massive nonfarm payroll losses coupled with a very high unemployment rate indicate a severe labor market crisis that depresses consumption and investment. The joint signal of low PAYEMS and high UNRATE predicts continued negative equity momentum over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the composite z-score of low PAYEMS and high UNRATE does not have a negative rank IC > 0.03 over a rolling 3-year window for 60-day returns.
